data.title: Docusign climbs higher on durable double-digit billings growth; analysts await better valuation
data.description:
Docusign (NASDAQ:DOCU) shares climbed 6% during early market action Friday following second quarter results and an outlook that topped estimates, but most analysts await a more compelling valuation.

Citizens maintained its Market Outperform rating and $124 price target.

"We continue to view DocuSign as an excellent opportunity for capital appreciation for several reasons, including it has the dominant e-signature franchise, with an incredible 1.7M customers, a strong brand, and very high customer satisfaction," said Citizens analysts led by Patrick Walravens in a Friday investor note. 

They also noted Docusign's $50B total addressable market, with $25B from e-signature and $25B from contract lifecycle management. The firm also highlighted DocuSign's artificial intelligence offering, IAM, where AI features help users process agreements.

Meanwhile, Bank of America reiterated its Neutral rating but increased its price target to $102 from $85.

"The guide for Q3 billings growth of 5% is below our admittedly high 7%, when considering a 7% point tougher comparison," said BofA analysts Brad Sills and Trevor Dodds, in a Friday note. "The FY26 outlook for billings moves marginally higher ($6 million). Our net takeaway on billings outlook is that with reasonable upside, DocuSign remains comfortably in the low double-digit billings growth range, a nice step up from low single digits in recent years. IAM is expected to contribute low double digit mix of billings, this year."

Piper Sandler also reiterated its Neutral rating and nudged its price target to $90 from $95.

"Improved NRR trends and further IAM traction are encouraging, but we are staying sidelined on shares waiting for more quarters of consistent execution before becoming more constructive," said Piper Sandler's Rob Owens in a note.

Similarly, RBC reiterated its Sector Perform rating and increased its price target to $95 from $90.

"Overall, it was a solid quarter with early signs of improving GTM execution and IAM traction," said RBC analysts led by Rishi Jaluria, in a note. "While valuation appears un-demanding, we await more positive data points on IAM before becoming more constructive."

Adobe (ADBE), which also offers digital signature services, was up 1% on Friday.

data.title: Wall Street's major averages slide after tagging record highs as payrolls data lands
data.description:
Wall Street’s broader market averages pare back earlier gains on Friday after all three major market averages notched record highs while yields slumped as the jobs report came in cooler than expected.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) trades near even. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is in the red by 0.2%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) has slid 0.3%.

From a sector-by-sector perspective, six of the 11 S&P segments are trading in positive territory, led by Materials. At the other end of the spectrum, Energy has lagged the most so far.

In the bond market, yields drop as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 7 basis points to 4.08%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down 10 basis points to 3.48%. Both the US2Y and US10Y have tagged 5-month lows.

On the economic front, August’s nonfarm payrolls report showed the economy added only 22,000 jobs—well below the 75,000 consensus forecast and trailing July’s 79,000 increase. At the same time, the unemployment rate ticked up to 4.3% from 4.2% in July, matching expectations.

"Friday's jobs report showed that employers are still hesitant to hire and is further confirmation that the labor market is weakening. This is the last jobs report before the Federal Reserve's September meeting and its weakness increases the chances that the Fed will proceed with its rate cut later this month," Rich Mullen, CEO, Pallas Capital Advisors stated.

Traders are now fully pricing in a rate cut in September after the latest labor report came in cooler than expected. Wall Street traded higher on Thursday as investors weighed fresh jobs data for clues on the labor market’s strength.

As for stocks that are on the move, Broadcom (AVGO) is up 10% after fiscal third quarter results beat estimates, and the company revealed a new customer, which saw bullish reactions from analysts.

data.title: Financials, REITs, small caps, and discretionary stocks poised to benefit as Fed cut rates
data.description:
Financial institutions and interest-sensitive sectors lead the list of stocks positioned to benefit immediately following a Federal Reserve interest rate cut.

With the Fed signaling increasing dovishness and the markets now pricing in an 84% probability of a 25-basis point cut in September, several companies are set to benefit immediately.

Fed Governor Christopher Waller expects "multiple cuts coming in" over the next three to six months, emphasizing the urgency as "when the labor market sours, it turns bad fast."

Rate cuts typically trigger investor re-evaluation of cyclical sectors like financials (NYSEARCA:XLF), (IYF). The broader market (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI) benefits from reduced competition from bonds on a securities' allocation level, while simultaneously making credit less expensive to support economic stability and corporate profit margins.

According to Seeking Alpha’s analytics, interest rate sensitive finance stocks screen as the prime beneficiaries of following a Fed rate cut:

  • Chime Financial (CHYM) – Down 33.2% year-to-date
  • SoFi Technologies (SOFI) – Up 63.9% YTD, Seeking Alpha Quant rating: 3.48
  • Sezzle (SEZL) – Up 99% YTD, SA Quant rating: 4.69
  • Qfin Holdings (QFIN) – Down 24% YTD, SA Quant rating: 3.09
  • Circle Internet Group (CRCL) – Up 35.4% YTD
  • Raymond James Financial (RJF) – Up 6.9% YTD, SA Quant rating: 2.86
  • Affirm Holdings (AFRM) – Up 49.3% YTD, SA Quant rating: 3.21
  • Fair Isaac (FICO) – Down 22.6% YTD, SA Quant rating: 2.96
  • Marqeta (MQ) – Up 63.3% YTD, SA Quant rating: 3.34
  • Dave (DAVE) – 124.2% YTD, SA Quant rating: 3.24

Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

Financial institutions lead the beneficiary list, since Fed cuts directly reduce banks’ funding costs while many of their loan portfolio remain at fixed rates. This creates immediate net interest margin expansion.  

Also, lower interest rates ease borrower payment burdens, reducing delinquency rates and credit losses across loan portfolios. Financial institutions with significant exposure to consumer lending particularly benefit from this dynamic, as reduced borrowing costs should "ease pressure" on credit card and auto loan payments. Rate cuts directly reduce their borrowing costs while potentially increasing lending demand.

Other sectors poised for immediate benefits include:

Real Estate Investment Trusts (XLRE), (SCHH), (REIT), with examples such as Prologis (PLD), a logistics real estate leader, as rate cuts reduce debt servicing costs while stimulating economic activity that drives demand for industrial properties.

Small-cap stocks (IWM), (SP600), (IJR) historically outperform during rate-cutting cycles, particularly international small-cap equities (SCHC), (AVDV), (FNDC). Lower interest rates make financing more attractive for smaller companies, where debt costs represent a larger portion of operations compared to large-cap peers.

Consumer discretionary stocks (XLY), (FDIS), (VCR) also benefit significantly as this sector tends to perform well around Fed rate cuts after a pause, as consumers gain increased spending power through lower borrowing costs.

Historical data shows that after Fed easing cycles, equities typically rise 7% over six months and 12% over twelve months – according to Seeking Alpha analyst group Manole Capital Management in a recent article – though sustainable rallies ultimately depend on earnings growth and economic fundamentals rather than Fed decisions alone.

data.title: Paramount Skydance initiated at Underperform by BofA
data.description:
Paramount Skydance's (NASDAQ:PSKY) post-merger roadmap is only just beginning to clear up -- and BofA Securities has initiated coverage of the stock at Underperform, expecting murky visibility through a long transition ahead.

"We believe PSKY has the potential to be a dynamic global media company," analyst Jessica Reif Ehrlich wrote. "However, there are no easy fixes and a turnaround such as this will take a significant amount of time, require substantial investment and investor patience."

"As evidenced with the Warner Bros and Discovery combination, the restructuring will likely take years to implement."

The multi-year exploration of a company sale, combined with regulatory overhang on the Skydance deal once it was proposed, meant Paramount Global ended up "starved of capital investment for years, as prior leadership focused on cost cuts and shorter-term performance," Reif Ehrlich said.

"With the right management and capital structure, we think PSKY has the potential to be a global media powerhouse driven by technology, creative and content leadership," she adds.

But the stock's currently trading at a rich multiple, she suggests, and there's limited visibility into operations for some time. November's earnings report will only begin to indicate the company's direction.

She's started PSKY with a price target of $11, implying 26% downside. Paramount Skydance stock (NASDAQ:PSKY) was up just over 1% Friday morning to $14.91.

data.title: WK Kellogg edges higher as FTC grants early termination for Ferrero sale
data.description:
• WK Kellogg (NYSE:KLG) ticked higher by 0.2% after the Federal Trade Commission granted early HSR termination for its planned sale to Ferrero, according to a posting on the regulator's website.
  • Ferrero Group agreed to acquire cereal giant WK Kellogg (NYSE:KLG) for $23 per share in cash in July, representing an enterprise value of $3.1B.

data.title: MGM Resorts International COO Corey Sanders announces retirement
data.description:
• MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM) chief operating officer Corey Sanders announced his retirement after a 30-year tenure at the entertainment and gaming company.
  • He currently oversees the company’s Las Vegas and regional properties, hospitality, gaming, human resources, and strategic initiatives.
  • Sanders will remain in his position until the end of the year and to serve as advisor to CEO William Hornbuckle until December 31, 2026.
  • MGM Resorts International (NYSE:MGM) will name Sanders’ successor later this month.

data.title: SEC, CFTC to hold roundtable to discuss 'regulatory harmonization'
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission and Commodity Futures Trading Commission will hold a joint roundtable on Sept. 29 to explore ways of aligning their rules, the two U.S. agencies said on Friday.

"To the extent possible and appropriate in the public interest under existing statutes, our respective agencies should consider harmonizing ; streamlining reporting and data standards; aligning capital and margin frameworks; and standing up coordinated innovation exemptions using each agency’s existing exemptive authority," according to a shared statement.

The move comes after the SEC and CFTC both confirmed a view that registered exchanges can in fact facilitate the trading of certain spot crypto asset products, including margin, leverage or financed so-called retail commodity transactions.

“Tuesday’s joint staff statement on spot crypto asset products is only a first step," said SEC Chairman Paul Atkins and CFTC Acting Chairman Caroline Pham in the statement. “By working in lockstep, our two agencies can harness our nation’s unique regulatory structure into a source of strength for market participants, investors and all Americans."

data.title: GCL Global signs term sheet to launch up to RMB150 mln game and cultural fund
data.description:
• GCL Global Holdings Ltd (NASDAQ:GCL) on Friday said it has signed a non-binding term sheet with Harmony Capital for the potential launch of a game and cultural industry fund in China, with a total budget of RMB100 million to RMB150 million.

  • The proposed fund would focus on strategic investments in game development, publishing, and broader cultural content, including film and television adaptations of game IPs and select pre-IPO opportunities, GCL said.

  • Over 70% of the fund’s capital is expected to be allocated to the interactive entertainment sector, according to Group CEO Sebastian Toke, who called the initiative a step toward long-term value creation.

  • The fund is planned to operate over a seven-year term, with a four-year investment period and a three-year exit period, subject to possible extension by unanimous approval.

  • "Our strategy is to drive growth by making direct equity investments in projects that promote creative and cultural content,” CEO Toke, said in a statement.
  • GCL is -1.4% to $3.155.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: REGENXBIO reports positive data from Hunter syndrome
data.description:
REGENXBIO (NASDAQ:RGNX) reported positive results from its trial investigating the gene therapy, clemidsogene lanparvovec (RGX-121), for Hunter syndrome treatment.

In the 12-month pivotal trial involving 13 participants, a significant finding was that after one year, there was an 82% median decrease in cerebrospinal fluid levels of heparan sulfate D2S6. 

In August 2025, the FDA conducted a pre-license inspection for the RGX-121 marketing application and reported no issues.

Throughout the review process, the company said that FDA has not identified any safety concerns, and RGX-121 has been well tolerated among all 26 patients treated in the trial as of August 20, 2024.

A decision from the FDA regarding the application is anticipated by February 8, 2026.
